About Quantaco

Hospitality moves fast the tools should keep up too. At Quantaco, we’re building technology that empowers hospitality businesses to work smarter, not harder. No more disconnected systems that slow you down and create extra work.

Australia’s only locally owned and operated Hospitality Management System, designed to simplify processes, save time, and deliver actionable insights for venues of any size.

Our suite of software and service solutions is optimised to work best together but designed to function independently, delivering the actionable insights and financial reporting pubs, clubs, and restaurants need to thrive.

We empower businesses through expert accounting services, real-time reporting, and connected hospitality operations tools. These tools simplify work, save time, and help venues make decisions that drive growth whether it’s one venue or one hundred.

About The Role

The primary purpose of the Software Developer is to manage the design, installation, testing and maintenance of software systems for our Cooking the Books (CTB) design suite.

This role will be responsible for excellent technical support and the development of software improvements for existing CTB programs or providing entirely new programs for release to the market.

What You’ll Do

Maintain and improve the performance of CTB’s existing software and maintain its’ websites, in-house hardware and network systems

Test, maintain and recommend software improvements to ensure strong functionality and optimisation

Review current systems and identify ideas to improve system performance and impact availability, including cost proposals

Facilitate root cause analysis of system issues and faults;

Resolve complex technical design issues;

Test new products or software improvement releases in a controlled environment before going live;

Proactively seek to continuously understand the design characteristics offered by key competitors; developing design concepts that surpass competitors;

Work closely with analysts, designers and staff to ensure programs operate effectively;

Assist with the preparation of training manuals for users;

Analyse user requirements and convert requirements to design documents;

Provide comprehensive support to internal customers, including achieving resolution to outstanding problems or issues;

Develop and implement new software;

Assist the Marketing department with preparation of digital design collateral in line with CTB’s brand guidelines;

Automate repetitive technical tasks

About You

You are organised and a fast learner

You have a mature, design-led approach to software development

Integrity, honesty, trust, and respect

Self-motivated, but you love working in a team

Innovative and always looking for ways to improve

You’re an effective communicator, patient, and able to explain your point of view

Have a degree in Computer Science or equivalent

Have 2+ years’ experience in C# and.Net Framework;

Possess strong technical knowledge of web technologies (Javascript, CSS, HTML, etc.);

Are proficient in C#, ASP.NET MVC and Web API

Have knowledge of ASP.NET Core, ADO.net and ORMs (EntityFramework, Dapper.net, NHibernate)

Have knowledge of Databases: SQL Server, PostgreSQL (Store Procedures, and NoSql: Redis and MongoGB)

Are proficient in JavaScript frameworks: AngularJs, VueJs, and ExtJs

Possess knowledge of Microsoft Azure cloud platform (App Service, Service Bus, Service Fabric)

Have experience with Source Code Control: SVN and GIT

Have a demonstrated understanding of projects from the perspective of both client and busines

Possess proficient verbal and written communication skills;

Have effective time management skills and the ability to meet deadlines

Have the ability to solve problems by being collaborative, creative and analytical

Why Join Us?

At Quantaco, we’re passionate about building technology that solves real-world problems in hospitality. Our team is collaborative, curious, and committed to making an impact and we want you to grow with us.

Reward: Competitive salary + up to 20% discretionary performance bonus
Wellbeing: Hybrid working, birthday leave, and wellness discounts through our benefits portal
Culture: Fortnightly collaboration days, social calendar, team events, peer recognition, and a strong commitment to inclusion
Growth: Study assistance, a structured development framework, and our Women at Quantaco initiative to support diverse career progression

Ready to build the future of hospitality tech?

Apply now or reach out for a confidential chat. We’d love to meet you.

About the job
At BAE Systems Australia 

Do interesting work that MATTERS
ENJOY what you do
GROW your career
Be YOU

We work in a thriving sector – one that puts us at the forefront of exciting and meaningful work. Every day we push boundaries to produce some of the world’s most innovative and groundbreaking technology. Individually we all contribute to our success. Collectively we make the impossible possible.

Recognised for delivering projects of global and national significance that help keep Australia safe, it is our shared passion that shapes our culture, and our people that make it a great place to work.

About The OPPORTUNITY

We have a great opportunity for multiple Software Engineers to join our Strategic Surveillance Program based at Adelaide – South Australia or Melbourne Central – Victoria, reporting to the Senior Software Engineer.

We embrace flexible work arrangements at BAE Systems Australia, such as working a 9-day fortnight and or hybrid working.

You will bring with you demonstrated previous skills and experience in the development of specialised software systems. Within our HF Export programs, you’ll work on areas such as UX development, SW design and architecture, and signal processing. We have a growing team with the opportunity to constantly learn and carve out your niche in an area that interests you. You’ll have interactions with many other teams and stakeholders, as well as engineers in other disciplines, and have opportunities to travel internationally. We’re a strong team that takes pride in what we do and how we work together, with a lot to offer a software engineer looking to expand their skillset.

Key responsibilities include

Conduct software engineering in accordance with the Software Development Plan
Writing high quality, maintainable software including generation of design and test documentation
Active participation in all Scrum ceremonies each sprint, including planning, stand-ups, reviews, and retrospectives, contributing to continuous improvement and team collaboration.

About YOU

Strong understanding of the Software Development lifecycle
Knowledge/experience in one or more of the following technologies:
Strong working knowledge of C/C++ Programming language (preferably on Linux)
Full Stack development with strong front-end and back-end development skills (HTML, CSS, JavaScript, React, or similar modern frameworks).
Experience with RESTful APIs, authentication, and third-party integrations.
Experience with a variety of communications libraries, e.g. Apache Kafka and DDS, in environments with very high data throughput
Expertise in Containers, Kubernetes
Strong working knowledge of programming on a Linux framework
Development experience with the Qt framework is desirable
Experience as part of an agile/scrum team
Familiarity with Configuration and Change Management principles and tools, such as Git
Strong analytical and troubleshooting skills
Defence security clearance or eligibility to obtain a NV1 clearance
Eligibility to be cleared for International Traffic In Arms (ITAR) regulations
We believe in hiring for potential, we know that some candidates hesitate if they don’t tick all the boxes. If this role interests you, but you don’t meet all the criteria, we still encourage you to apply.

About US

You will be joining a workplace that truly cares about your wellbeing.

Our people are diverse and we value the variety of perspectives this brings, we know that a culture that is inclusive and fosters a sense of belonging is a place we can all make the most of our unique talents and be our best.

As Australia’s largest defence company in a rapidly growing sector, you will be connected into a global team and encouraged to explore where your career might take you whether that is a traditional career path or squiggly one, there are infinite possibilities for you at BAE Systems Australia.

BAE Systems recognises the sacrifices of those who have served our Nation and their families. We support our veteran community through our internal veteran network (VetNet Australia) and strong charitable partnerships, internal offerings including reserve service leave, a Veteran Mentoring Program and other exclusive offerings available through our strategic partners.

We welcome applicants from all people regardless of sex, gender identity, race, ethnicity, disability, pregnancy, age, or other lived experience; and strongly encourage applications from Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people for all positions.

We are proud to be recognised as an employer of choice for women by WORK180.

Awarded the 2024 Quarter 1 Benchmarcx Triple R- Excellence In Candidate Experience Certification. As a member of the Circle Back Initiative we commit to respond to every applicant.

To learn more about our competitive employee benefits, flexibility, other employment opportunities and what to expect from our recruitment process please visit: www.baesystemscareers.com.au

Clearances

About the job
About Zero to Claw
Zero to Claw is a white-glove OpenClaw setup and automation service. We deploy security-hardened, production-ready OpenClaw AI agents for businesses. Our clients range from solo operators who want a tireless digital assistant to small teams who need agents handling email triage, meeting prep, CRM workflows, and cross-app automation.
Our founders bring deep experience: Jake has led $100M+ in business transformation, and Daniel is a former Atlassian Engineer with 6+ years shipping Jira, Bitbucket, and Confluence to millions of users.

The Role
We need someone to help us deliver custom OpenClaw workflows for our clients and maintain their running instances. You'll be the person who takes a client's messy "I want my AI to do X when Y happens" request, figures out how to wire it up in OpenClaw, builds it, tests it, and makes sure it keeps running. You'll also handle troubleshooting when things break — a misconfigured MCP server, a flaky WhatsApp integration, a gateway auth issue, an agent that's burning through API credits. You'll be deploying, configuring, integrating, and debugging real systems for real clients on real deadlines.

What you'll do
Connect OpenClaw to client tools — Gmail, Notion, Slack, Jira, Salesforce, databases, and hundreds more
Build custom workflows and skills when off-the-shelf integrations don't cut it
Troubleshoot live client instances — broken integrations, runaway API costs, flaky messaging channels, agent misbehaviour
Talk to clients, understand what they need, and translate that into working automation

What will help you succeed
Comfort in a terminal — SSH, config files, logs, basic server admin
Some experience with any programming language — enough to read code, write scripts, and hit APIs
Basic understanding of networking — ports, firewalls, DNS
Familiarity with Docker
Clear communication — you'll explain technical things to non-technical people regularly
A bias toward figuring things out over waiting to be taught

Bonus points
You've played with OpenClaw, MCP, or any AI agent framework
You've deployed something to a VPS or cloud instance before
You know your way around monitoring tools (Langfuse, Grafana, etc.)
You have opinions about LLMs, tokens, and model routing
You've built a bot, a home server, a Raspberry Pi project, or anything that proves you like tinkering

You don't need to tick every box. If you're curious, resourceful, and excited about AI agents — we'd rather hear from you than not.

Why join us
You'll get production experience with AI agents that most people only read about. You'll work directly with two founders — one from Atlassian engineering, one from enterprise strategy — who'll give you real responsibility from day one, not busywork. And you'll be joining at the ground floor of a company in one of the fastest-moving spaces in tech.

To apply
Send an email to join@zerotoclaw.ai with a short note about why this interests you, your resume/LinkedIn, and ideally one example of something technical you've built or deployed (a side project, a home server, a bot, anything that shows you can ship).

About the job
About Fat Zebra
At Fat Zebra we simplify payments so that merchants and platforms can scale with confidence. Our flexible, secure, and scalable payments platform supports over 100,000 merchants across Australia and New Zealand. We help our customers navigate the complexity of the payments ecosystem so that they can focus on what matters most - growing their business.

About The Opportunity
As a fast-growing payments scale-up, we’re continuously evolving our technology and investing in the future. The Data & AI team is building a new, modern data platform to deliver observability and insights to our internal and external customers. We have a new role for a Data Engineer to add to our foundation team; someone who can turbo charge our capacity for ingesting events, building data products, and providing data insights to our consumers. As we progress with our strategy, there will also be opportunities to develop analytic and agentic capabilities to layer on top of the foundational data products.

As a key member of our Data & AI team, you will contribute and collaborate on the following:
Build and maintain robust data pipelines from source systems into lakes and warehouses.
Negotiate and develop processes and data contracts for data pipelines.
Deliver curated, API-ready data products.
Develop opinions about architecture and ways of working.
Reconcile data sources with transformed data products.
Implement best practices for coding standards, CI/CD, and testing.
This is a hands-on role with real impact and is ideal for someone who has a passion for data and wants to shape a data platform from the ground up while contributing to a broader Data & AI strategy.

In addition, you will have the opportunity to contribute to analytics, insights, and emerging agentic capabilities that optimise services for our merchants and customers.

About You
We are looking for someone with a product mindset and big aspirations. Someone who is curious, driven, and excited to build something that is future focused and benefits merchants, customers and Fat Zebra. Someone who:
is excited by the challenge of creating new architecture and pipelines,
has a passion for building in a testable and extensible way,
understands cloud data infrastructure as it relates to scalable event ingestion and processing,
is comfortable with event‑driven architectures and streaming or near‑real‑time workloads, and
has a bachelor’s degree or equivalent years of work experience in a field like Computer Science, Software Engineering, Data Science, Data Engineering, Networking & Cloud Computing.

Required Skills
Experience with Python, SQL, Typescript, and event-based systems at scale
Working knowledge of AWS and cloud-based data infrastructure
Experience building and maintaining robust data pipelines
Understanding of modern data architectures (OLTP/OLAP, pipelines, streaming)
Familiarity with CI/CD, testing practices, and infrastructure as code.

Nice To Have
Experience with event-based systems at scale
Experience in the payments industry
Experience with Ruby on Rails
An interest in building agentic systems to deliver factual insights over real data.

Why Join Fat Zebra?
Be part of a fast-growing Australian Payments company scale-up
Work on a greenfield data platform transformation
Hybrid working arrangements
Supportive, people-first culture
Opportunity to shape the future of Data & AI at Fat Zebra.

Curious? Read the PD, ask questions we’d love to hear from you.

For a confidential conversation please contact Talent Manager, Rose Maugeri on 0409236061 at Fat Zebra.
